title: PT Study SOP - Remaining Fixes
description: Fix all remaining issues to make the full study loop work end to end.

stories.title: Polish panel end-to-end verification and fixes
stories.description: The Polish panel is wired in TutorShell but has not been tested end to end. Verify it works: (1) Open Polish panel from toolbar. (2) It should show tutor replies, notes, summaries, and cards staged for export. (3) User should be able to review and edit captured content. (4) If any of this is broken or placeholder, fix it so Polish actually displays session artifacts. Trace from TutorShell polishStudioContent through to whatever component renders the Polish panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Polish panel opens from toolbar
Polish panel shows captured session content (tutor replies, notes)
Content is editable or reviewable
dev-browser: open Polish panel, verify it has real content after a priming run

stories.title: Polish Packet shows tutor outputs staged for export
stories.description: The Polish Packet panel should display tutor replies, promoted notes, summaries, and Anki card drafts. Verify it works after running a tutor chat session. If it shows placeholder text or is empty, trace the data flow from tutor chat responses through promotedPolishPacketNotes and fix it.
acceptanceCriteria: Polish Packet panel opens from toolbar
After a tutor chat session, Polish Packet shows tutor responses
Content can be reviewed before export
dev-browser: open Polish Packet, verify content after tutor chat

stories.title: Notes panel accepts and persists freeform text
stories.description: The Notes panel should let the user type freeform notes during a study session. Open Notes panel from toolbar. Verify it has a text area. Type text. Verify text persists on panel resize and does not disappear. Notes should be associated with the current session.
acceptanceCriteria: Notes panel opens from toolbar
Notes panel has a text input area (textarea or rich editor)
User can type text that persists
Text survives panel resize
dev-browser: open Notes, type text, verify it stays

stories.title: Document Dock clip tool supports image paste and screenshot snip
stories.description: The Document Dock has a selective passage clip feature but it only accepts text input. Add: (1) Paste support -- user should be able to Ctrl+V an image from clipboard into the clip area. Use the Clipboard API (navigator.clipboard.read()) to detect image data and render it as an inline image in the workspace. (2) If full snipping tool is too complex, at minimum add a note that says 'Paste an image with Ctrl+V' and handle the paste event on the clip input area. (3) The clipped content (text or image) should be promotable to the workspace canvas. Check the existing handleClipExcerpt function in TutorShell for the current clip flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Clip area accepts pasted images via Ctrl+V
Pasted images render as inline previews
Both text and image clips can be sent to workspace
dev-browser: verify clip area has paste handler for images

stories.title: End session flow saves to vault and cleans up
stories.description: When a study session ends (user clicks End Session or NEW SESSION), the app should: (1) Save session notes and artifacts to the Obsidian vault folder for that course. (2) Show a brief summary of what was saved. (3) Clean up session state. (4) Return to the entry card for the next session. Trace the end session flow from handleTutorSessionAction in tutor.tsx. Check if vault save logic exists in the session or workflow hooks. If not, add a basic save-to-vault step that writes a markdown summary to the vault folder.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking NEW SESSION or End Session triggers save flow
Session artifacts are written to the vault folder as markdown
User sees confirmation of what was saved
State is cleaned up and entry card shows
dev-browser: end a session, verify save confirmation

stories.title: Obsidian panel shows vault browser with note read and write
stories.description: The Obsidian panel is currently a placeholder. Replace with a real vault browser that: (1) Shows the folder tree for the current course vault folder (derived from hub.vaultFolder or COURSE_FOLDERS config). (2) Lets user click a note to read it in a preview pane. (3) Has a Create Note button that creates a new markdown note in the vault. (4) Has a Save to Vault button that saves current session notes to the vault. The vault path comes from the Obsidian vault at C:\Users\treyt\Desktop\Treys School. Use the existing filesystem API or a backend endpoint to read/write vault files. If no backend endpoint exists for vault operations, create one in brain/dashboard/ that reads and writes markdown files from the vault path.
acceptanceCriteria: Obsidian panel shows folder tree for current course
Clicking a note shows its content in preview
Create Note button creates a new markdown file
Save to Vault button saves session notes
dev-browser: open Obsidian panel, verify folder tree renders

stories.title: Anki panel shows card preview and export
stories.description: The Anki panel is currently a placeholder. Replace with a real panel that: (1) Shows Anki cards generated from the current session (from Polish Packet or tutor responses). (2) Each card shows front/back preview. (3) User can edit cards before export. (4) Export button writes cards as a CSV or APKG file that can be imported into Anki. (5) If Anki export is too complex for now, at minimum show the card previews and provide a Copy to Clipboard button for each card. Check if there is an existing Anki export utility in the codebase -- search for anki, flashcard, or card export.
acceptanceCriteria: Anki panel opens from toolbar
Panel shows generated flashcards with front/back preview
Cards are editable
Export or copy button exists for getting cards out
dev-browser: open Anki panel, verify card previews render

stories.title: Entry card scrolls on small viewports
stories.description: On smaller screens or when the browser window is not maximized, the entry card form may overflow the viewport and get cut off at the bottom. The entry card overlay should have overflow-y-auto with max-height so the form scrolls if it exceeds the viewport height. Add max-h-[90vh] overflow-y-auto to the entry card container inside the overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Entry card is scrollable when it exceeds viewport height
All form fields and buttons are reachable by scrolling
Works on viewport heights as small as 600px
dev-browser: resize to 800x600, verify entry card scrolls
